comment_text.sn1.77:1.4: “Pest” is abbuda, literally “tumor”, “cancer”. Also an early stage of embryonic development (SN 10.1:3.2) and the name of a hell or a very large number (Snp 3.10:8.2, SN 6.10:6.2). Originally the name of the snake Arbuda crushed beneath Indra’s foot, where he is mentioned along with other foes such as Vritra, Namuci, etc. (Rig Veda 1.51.6, 2.11.20, 2.14.4, 8.3.19, 8.32.3, 8.32.26, 10.67.12). As Arbuda Kādraveya he is, rather curiously, said to have composed Rig Veda 10.94, where he addresses the pressing stones. He is also invoked in Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 13.4.3.9.

translation_text.sn1.77:1.3: What in the world is a rusty sword?
translation_text.sn1.77:1.4: Who is a pest in the world?
translation_text.sn1.77:2.1: Who gets arrested when they take things away?
translation_text.sn1.77:2.2: And who is loved when they take things away?
translation_text.sn1.77:2.3: And who is approved by the astute
translation_text.sn1.77:2.4: when they come again and again?”
translation_text.sn1.77:3.2: A woman is the best of valuables.
translation_text.sn1.77:3.3: Anger in the world is a rusty sword.
translation_text.sn1.77:3.4: A bandit is a pest in the world.
translation_text.sn1.77:4.1: A bandit gets arrested when they take things away.
translation_text.sn1.77:4.2: And an ascetic is loved when they take things away.
translation_text.sn1.77:4.3: An ascetic is approved by the astute
translation_text.sn1.77:4.4: when they come again and again.”
